questionHtml: Explain how marine mammals are adapted for movement in water.
markschemeHtml:
1. front limbs modified to flippers provide a large surface area/streamlined for steering;
  2. tail has flukes to increase the thrust when moved up and down;
  3. streamlined body/ skin without hair allows swift movement as resistance to water is minimized;
  4. presence of blowhole at the top of the head allows them to breathe quickly and easily when they rise to the surface;
  5. no connection between mouth and trachea/ airways prevent water entering the lungs;

meta.title: Explain how marine mammals are adapted for movement in water.
breadcrumbLabels: B3.3.10. Adaptations for swimming in marine mammals
